title: Workers
description: Declare and place you-agent-factory workers: runtime executors bound by workstations, named in factory.json, with backend details in workers/<name>/AGENTS.md.
openingSummary: Workers are the runtime executors that workstation steps invoke. factory.json names each worker; the matching workers/<name>/AGENTS.md file supplies the backend and shared system instructions.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: This reference covers you-agent-factory workers: what a worker is, how it sits beside workstations and factory.json, which public worker type matches a backend, where fields belong in the worker-vs-workstation split, and enough core fields plus a minimal example to author a worker. Use it when you need the durable web workers contract for placement, identity, ownership, and type selection.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: A worker is a runtime executor that a workstation invokes by name. factory.json declares each worker name in the factory topology. The worker directory at workers/<name>/AGENTS.md holds the backend type and shared system instructions for every workstation that binds that name. A workstation owns step routing, prompt body, limits, and outputs; the worker owns the execution backend. Keep worker definitions in the split layout beside factory.json under workers/<name>/AGENTS.md.

workerTaxonomy.title: Worker Taxonomy
workerTaxonomy.body: Public factory config names workers by behavior class. Use the matrix below to pick the current public type for a new worker. Prefer these names in new configs; legacy aliases remain only for migration.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Author a worker in workers/<name>/AGENTS.md, declare the same name in factory.json, and bind it from a workstation with the workstation worker field. Put backend identity on the worker and step routing on the workstation. Only type is required for a split worker definition when operator defaults supply model identity. Use the ownership matrix, minimal agent example, and type guidance below to place fields correctly.

coreFields.title: Core Fields
coreFields.body: Use this field reference when authoring a worker. It covers the fields you need to choose a type and supply backend identity. It is not a full flag dump.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is a web workers reference for types, placement, ownership, and core authoring. It is not a sync of packaged CLI docs (you docs workers), not a workstation routing or lifecycle guide, not a resource capacity deep-dive, and not a full agent failure-class catalog. Sibling configuration, workstations, and resources pages own those surfaces when published. This page still defines what a worker is on its own; sibling links are discovery only.

links.taxonomyInferenceWhen: Harnessless inference such as TTS, ASR, or typed INFERENCE_RUN dispatches
links.taxonomyAgentWhen: Prompt-rendered AGENT_RUN workstations that iterate until acceptance, rejection, or failure
links.taxonomyPollerWhen: Workstations with behavior POLLER that ingest external work through built-in providers
links.taxonomyLegacyNote: Legacy compatibility aliases: MODEL_WORKER still loads and projects to INFERENCE_WORKER or AGENT_WORKER depending on the paired workstation; HOSTED_WORKER still loads and projects to POLLER_WORKER. Prefer the current public names in new configs.
links.ownershipWorkstationRouting: type, worker, promptFile, prompt body, openCodeAgent
links.ownershipWorkerPoller: provider, auth.secretRef, linear.* for hosted pollers
links.ownershipWorkerBody: Worker body used as the model system prompt
links.ownershipWorkstationPrompt: Prompt template used as the rendered user message
links.minimalExampleLabel: Minimal agent worker (only type is required when operator defaults supply model identity):
links.minimalAgentExample:
---
type: AGENT_WORKER
---

You are a helpful assistant.
links.typeGuidanceInference: INFERENCE_WORKER — declare operations and one-shot model identity for INFERENCE_RUN; do not use operations as a substitute for an agent loop.
links.typeGuidanceAgent: AGENT_WORKER — put the shared system prompt in the markdown body; do not declare operations (validation rejects capability declarations on agent workers).
links.typeGuidanceScript: SCRIPT_WORKER — set command and args; the markdown body is descriptive only and is not executed.
links.typeGuidancePoller: POLLER_WORKER — set provider and auth.secretRef (never inline secrets); keep behavior POLLER and routing on the workstation.
links.coreModelControls: Concrete model identifier such as gpt-5-codex
links.coreModelProviderControls: Model-routing provider identity (built-in values include CLAUDE and CODEX)
links.coreProviderControls: Built-in hosted provider such as LINEAR
links.coreAuthControls: Referenced secret name resolved at runtime (never an inline key)
links.coreOperationsControls: Provider-agnostic one-shot capability declarations with typed input and output slots
